arguments.task: Research Zoho Cliq using official first-party sources only where possible. Open Zoho Cliq main site, then find and extract evidence from official developer/API/auth docs, pricing or plans pages, support/help pages, changelog/release notes if relevant, and any official GitHub or MCP registry pages if available. Capture URLs and short exact supporting text for: one-line product description, available API/auth methods, whether access is self-serve or gated and why, API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None) and breadth, rate limit notes, and any official MCP/server availability. Also verify if toolkit buildability is ready today or blocked by access/payment/documentation limits. Return a concise structured summary with source URLs for each claim.

input.thought: Start browser-based first-party research for Zoho Cliq official docs and access details.

raw_result.description: Team chat and collaboration software for business communication with internal teams and external stakeholders.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Zoho Cliq REST API v2 documentation indicates OAuth 2.0 authentication for API access. No other public API auth method was verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.gating_reason: Zoho Cliq has a publicly accessible product site, public pricing page with a Free plan, and publicly accessible REST API documentation without an apparent contact-sales or partner gate in the gathered evidence.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API v2 with JSON responses and documented endpoints including core collaboration objects plus Networks/external collaboration support; exact endpoint breadth was not exhaustively verified from gathered pages.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Zoho Cliq MCP server or official MCP registry/GitHub evidence was verified in the gathered evidence set.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official gathered evidence supports existence of rate limiting via HTTP 429 in the REST API error documentation, but no numeric rate-limit quotas were verified.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official first-party docs show a public REST API v2, JSON API root at cliq.zoho.com/api/v2, OAuth 2.0 auth, and a Free pricing tier. Error docs referencing 429 suggest production API governance. Product messaging explicitly includes external stakeholder collaboration.
raw_result.human_review_reason: MCP availability was not exhaustively checked beyond the gathered evidence, exact numeric rate limits were not found, and the self-serve buildability conclusion relies on public docs/pricing rather than a directly verified developer app-registration flow page.

result.description: Team chat and collaboration software for business communication with internal teams and external stakeholders.
result.auth_notes: Official Zoho Cliq REST API v2 documentation indicates OAuth 2.0 authentication for API access. No other public API auth method was verified from the gathered evidence.
result.gating_reason: Zoho Cliq has a publicly accessible product site, public pricing page with a Free plan, and publicly accessible REST API documentation without an apparent contact-sales or partner gate in the gathered evidence.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API v2 with JSON responses and documented endpoints including core collaboration objects plus Networks/external collaboration support; exact endpoint breadth was not exhaustively verified from gathered pages.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Zoho Cliq MCP server or official MCP registry/GitHub evidence was verified in the gathered evidence set.

result.rate_limit_notes: Official gathered evidence supports existence of rate limiting via HTTP 429 in the REST API error documentation, but no numeric rate-limit quotas were verified.
result.notable_signals: Official first-party docs show a public REST API v2, JSON API root at cliq.zoho.com/api/v2, OAuth 2.0 auth, and a Free pricing tier. Error docs referencing 429 suggest production API governance. Product messaging explicitly includes external stakeholder collaboration.
result.human_review_reason: MCP availability was not exhaustively checked beyond the gathered evidence, exact numeric rate limits were not found, and the self-serve buildability conclusion relies on public docs/pricing rather than a directly verified developer app-registration flow page.
